category.label: Number Systems and Properties
category.description: Properties of real numbers, number-set classification, arithmetic operations, and numeric structure. Builds the fluency with signed numbers, fractions, percents, and order-of-operations that every downstream algebra and precalculus topic assumes.
category.yield_rationale: Ubiquitous prerequisite: signed-number and fraction fluency are load-bearing for every downstream category, but number-systems content is rarely the direct test object once functions are introduced.

umbrellas.label: Real Number Set Membership
umbrellas.description: The standard real-number subsets (ℕ, ℤ, ℚ, irrational, ℝ) and the containment chain among them — what each set contains and how they nest.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Set-definition and containment vocabulary is background literacy; only rarely the direct exam question once functions are in play.

in_depth.label: Natural numbers
in_depth.description: Tests whether a student correctly identifies the counting numbers {1, 2, 3, …}, excluding 0, negatives, fractions, and irrationals.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Rarely tested independently; foundational vocabulary only.

in_depth.label: Whole numbers
in_depth.description: Tests the distinction between whole numbers {0, 1, 2, …} and natural numbers, specifically the inclusion of 0.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Minimal standalone test value; distinguished from natural numbers by a single element.

in_depth.label: Integers
in_depth.description: Tests identification of the set of positive whole numbers, negative whole numbers, and 0, excluding non-integer fractions and decimals.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Needed to classify signed numbers correctly; moderate background value.

in_depth.label: Rational numbers
in_depth.description: Tests recognition that any number expressible as a ratio of two integers (nonzero denominator) is rational, including integers, terminating decimals, and repeating decimals.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Rational vs. irrational distinction is prerequisite for exact-value work in trig and logarithm contexts.

in_depth.label: Irrational numbers
in_depth.description: Tests identification of real numbers whose decimal expansion is nonterminating and nonrepeating, and that cannot be written as a ratio of integers.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Distinguishing rational from irrational is prerequisite for exact-form trig values and logarithm evaluation.

in_depth.label: Real numbers
in_depth.description: Tests that any number placeable on the number line is real, contrasting with non-real complex numbers (which are out of scope here).
in_depth.yield_rationale: Serves as the overarching set; useful mainly for domain-and-range discussions in downstream categories.

in_depth.label: Containment of real-number sets
in_depth.description: Tests the nested containment ℕ ⊂ ℤ_≥0 ⊂ ℤ ⊂ ℚ ⊂ ℝ with irrational ⊂ ℝ but irrational ∩ ℚ = ∅, requiring students to place sets in a correct containment diagram.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Containment reasoning is a one-time conceptual hurdle; not revisited heavily downstream.

umbrellas.label: Real Number Classification Skills
umbrellas.description: Applying set-membership knowledge to classify a given number — identifying all sets it belongs to, finding the most specific set, and using decimal-form cues to determine rationality or irrationality.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Classification skill (most-specific set, rational vs. irrational from decimal form) appears more often than raw set-definition questions at AP level.

in_depth.label: Multiple membership in number sets
in_depth.description: Tests listing all real-number subsets that contain a given number rather than only the smallest set.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Complement skill to 'most specific classification'; low direct exam weight.

in_depth.label: Most specific number-set classification
in_depth.description: Tests selecting the smallest standard subset (e.g., natural rather than rational) that describes a given number — the canonical classification move.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Most common form of number-classification MC question; still low overall weight.

in_depth.label: Rational decimal forms
in_depth.description: Tests recognizing that every rational number's decimal expansion either terminates or eventually repeats, used to classify decimals without converting to fractions.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Supports exact-vs-approximate distinctions in log and trig exact-value contexts.

in_depth.label: Irrational decimal forms
in_depth.description: Tests recognizing irrationality from a decimal that continues without any repeating block, and distinguishing it from terminating or repeating decimals.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Paired with rational_decimal_forms; same limited standalone weight.

in_depth.label: Perfect-square integer roots
in_depth.description: Tests number-set classification of √n when n is a perfect square (result is an integer), NOT radical simplification.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Appears as classification in exact-value trig questions; boundary with exponents_and_radicals is purely classification here.

in_depth.label: Non-perfect-square irrational roots
in_depth.description: Tests classifying √n as irrational when n is a positive integer that is not a perfect square, focusing on classification only, not rewriting the radical.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Prerequisite recognition for exact-form answers; boundary skill before radical manipulation in F6.

umbrellas.label: Fraction and Decimal Representations
umbrellas.description: Place value, expanded form, equivalent fractions, reducing fractions, mixed numbers and improper fractions, and converting terminating decimals to fractions.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Fraction-equivalence and lowest-terms skills are directly load-bearing for rational expression work in F5/P1; decimal place value and expanded form have lower AP-level weight.

in_depth.label: Decimal place value
in_depth.description: Tests identifying the value of a digit based on its position (tenths, hundredths, thousandths, etc.) relative to the decimal point.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Background skill; needed for decimal arithmetic alignment but not independently tested downstream.

in_depth.label: Expanded notation for numbers
in_depth.description: Tests writing a number as the sum of its place-value parts (numeric decomposition without variables).
in_depth.yield_rationale: Low downstream relevance; primarily a primary/middle-school skill.

in_depth.label: Equivalent fraction forms
in_depth.description: Tests recognizing that scaling numerator and denominator by the same nonzero factor produces an equivalent fraction.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Equivalent fraction reasoning underlies LCD-finding and rational expression simplification in F5/P1.

in_depth.label: Fractions in lowest terms
in_depth.description: Tests reducing a fraction to lowest terms by dividing numerator and denominator by their GCF.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Required for rational function simplification and partial-fraction work in P1.

in_depth.label: Improper fractions and mixed numbers
in_depth.description: Tests converting between improper fraction form and mixed number form for rational numbers ≥ 1.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Occasional in numeric computation; less critical once variable fractions dominate.

in_depth.label: Terminating decimal fraction forms
in_depth.description: Tests converting a terminating decimal to a fraction with a power-of-10 denominator, then reducing.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Supports rational number identification and exact-value reasoning.

umbrellas.label: Decimal, Percent, and Notation Forms
umbrellas.description: Converting repeating decimals to fractions, fraction-to-decimal conversion, fraction-decimal-percent equivalence, scientific notation, and distinguishing exact from approximate representations.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Percent-decimal fluency and exact-vs-approximate reasoning are directly exercised in exponential modeling (P2) and no-calculator AP exam sections.

in_depth.label: Repeating decimal fraction forms
in_depth.description: Tests converting a repeating decimal to a fraction using the repeating-block algebraic trick.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Confirms rationality of repeating decimals; less frequent than terminating conversion.

in_depth.label: Fraction-to-decimal forms
in_depth.description: Tests converting a fraction to its decimal equivalent (terminating or repeating) by long division.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Needed for mixed-form comparisons and percent/decimal interconversion.

in_depth.label: Fraction-decimal-percent equivalence
in_depth.description: Tests representing the same rational quantity in three forms — fraction, decimal, percent — without applied percent modeling.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Percent-decimal fluency is directly required for interpreting exponential growth/decay multipliers in P2.

in_depth.label: Scientific notation form
in_depth.description: Tests recognizing and writing a number in scientific notation a × 10^n where 1 ≤ |a| < 10; representation only, no exponent-law arithmetic.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Scientific notation appears in semi-log contexts (AP Precalc 2.15) and order-of-magnitude estimation.

in_depth.label: Standard form from scientific notation
in_depth.description: Tests converting a number written in scientific notation back to ordinary decimal notation by interpreting the sign and magnitude of the power of 10.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Inverse of scientific-notation form; similar downstream weight.

in_depth.label: Exact and approximate number forms
in_depth.description: Tests distinguishing exact representations (fractions, radicals, π) from approximations (rounded decimals), and identifying which form is exact for a given quantity.
in_depth.yield_rationale: No-calculator sections of AP Precalc and Calc AB require exact-form answers; distinguishing exact from approximate is a recurring exam literacy skill.

umbrellas.label: Divisibility Rules and Parity
umbrellas.description: Even and odd integers, parity of sums and products, digit-based divisibility rules (by 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10, 12), prime and composite numbers.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Divisibility rules and parity are low-weight background knowledge at AP level; they support prime factorization and GCF/LCM but are rarely directly tested.

in_depth.label: Even and odd integers
in_depth.description: Tests classifying an integer as even (divisible by 2) or odd, and applying parity as a property of integers.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Background parity reasoning; not a direct AP Precalc or Calc AB test object.

in_depth.label: Parity of sums and products
in_depth.description: Tests using even-odd rules to determine the parity of sums and products (e.g., odd + odd = even) without variables.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Low standalone weight; parity reasoning supports polynomial symmetry concepts at a background level.

in_depth.label: Divisibility by 2, 5, and 10
in_depth.description: Tests applying last-digit divisibility rules to determine if an integer is divisible by 2, 5, or 10.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Quick-recognition rules; minimal downstream AP relevance.

in_depth.label: Divisibility by 3 and 9
in_depth.description: Tests the digit-sum rules for divisibility by 3 and 9, distinct from final-digit tests.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Digit-sum rule; low AP-level weight.

in_depth.label: Divisibility by 4 and 8
in_depth.description: Tests applying last-two or last-three digit rules for divisibility by 4 and 8.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Rarely tested beyond arithmetic contexts.

in_depth.label: Divisibility by 6 and 12
in_depth.description: Tests composite divisibility rules (satisfying multiple simpler conditions simultaneously, e.g., divisible by both 2 and 3 for divisibility by 6).
in_depth.yield_rationale: Combined-condition logic is useful for LCM reasoning; minimal direct exam weight.

in_depth.label: Prime and composite numbers
in_depth.description: Tests classifying positive integers greater than 1 as prime (exactly two factors) or composite (more than two), including the special status of 1.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Prerequisite for prime factorization; low direct test weight at AP level.

umbrellas.label: Prime Factorization, GCF, and LCM
umbrellas.description: Factors and multiples, prime factorization, greatest common factor, least common multiple, relatively prime integers, and remainders after integer division.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: GCF and LCM are the direct mechanisms for fraction reduction and LCD-finding (rational_number_arithmetic); prime factorization and remainders feed polynomial GCF factoring in F5.

in_depth.label: Factors and multiples
in_depth.description: Tests identifying the factors of an integer and listing its multiples, establishing the base idea behind divisibility.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Factor recognition is the entry move for prime factorization, GCF, and polynomial GCF factoring.

in_depth.label: Prime factorization
in_depth.description: Tests writing a positive integer as a product of prime factors, which is the mechanism behind GCF and LCM computation.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Prime factorization is the computational tool for numeric GCF/LCM, which feed polynomial GCF factoring and rational simplification.

in_depth.label: Greatest common factor of integers
in_depth.description: Tests finding the largest integer that divides two or more given integers evenly (numeric GCF only, no variables).
in_depth.yield_rationale: Numeric GCF directly supports reducing fractions and is the conceptual basis for polynomial GCF factoring in F5.

in_depth.label: Least common multiple of integers
in_depth.description: Tests finding the smallest positive integer that is a multiple of two or more integers, supporting common-denominator arithmetic.
in_depth.yield_rationale: LCM is the mechanism for finding LCD in fraction arithmetic; moderate downstream load.

in_depth.label: Relatively prime integers
in_depth.description: Tests determining whether two integers share no common factor greater than 1 (GCF = 1), without requiring either number to be prime itself.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Supports lowest-terms reasoning and coprimality in modular contexts; low direct AP weight.

in_depth.label: Remainders after integer division
in_depth.description: Tests expressing integer division with a quotient and a nonnegative remainder smaller than the divisor, connecting to the Division Algorithm.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Remainder concept feeds polynomial long-division Remainder Theorem in F5; minimal direct AP weight here.

umbrellas.label: Signed Number Concepts
umbrellas.description: Classifying positive, negative, and zero; opposites; absolute value as magnitude; number-line distance; midpoint; and sign rules for products and quotients.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Sign fluency — including absolute value, distance, and product/quotient sign rules — is load-bearing in function evaluation, transformation parameters, and trig value interpretation throughout AP Precalc.

in_depth.label: Positive numbers, negative numbers, and zero
in_depth.description: Tests classifying a number as positive, negative, or zero, and that zero is neither positive nor negative.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Sign classification is the entry skill for all signed arithmetic; moderate standalone weight.

in_depth.label: Opposite numbers
in_depth.description: Tests identifying the additive opposite of a number (same distance from 0, opposite side of number line) and locating opposites on a number line.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Opposite-number fluency supports reflection transformations and additive inverse property throughout algebra.

in_depth.label: Absolute value as magnitude
in_depth.description: Tests evaluating the absolute value of a number as its nonnegative distance from 0; numeric only, not absolute value equations or functions.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Absolute value is a prerequisite for distance between numbers, and the concept reappears as a function in piecewise and transformation contexts.

in_depth.label: Distance between numbers on the number line
in_depth.description: Tests computing the distance between two real numbers as |a − b|, applied to numeric values only (no coordinate-plane distance).
in_depth.yield_rationale: Distance reasoning is used in sinusoidal parameter extraction (midline = average of max/min) and interval-length calculations.

in_depth.label: Midpoint between two real numbers
in_depth.description: Tests finding the midpoint of two numbers on a number line as their average — one-dimensional only, not coordinate geometry.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Midpoint of max and min values gives sinusoidal midline; moderate load in AP Precalc Unit 3.

in_depth.label: Sign of products
in_depth.description: Tests determining the sign of a product from the count of negative factors: even count → positive, odd count → negative.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Sign-of-product rule is used constantly in polynomial evaluation, derivative computation, and trig expression simplification.

umbrellas.label: Signed Number Arithmetic Rules
umbrellas.description: Sign rules for quotients; signed addition with same and opposite signs; subtraction as adding the opposite; and negative signs outside grouping symbols.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Signed arithmetic rules — especially subtract-as-add-opposite and negating a group — are direct error sources in algebraic manipulation and function evaluation throughout all downstream categories.

in_depth.label: Sign of quotients
in_depth.description: Tests determining that a quotient is positive when dividend and divisor share the same sign, negative when they differ; division by zero treated separately.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Sign of quotients underlies rational function sign analysis and limit sign reasoning.

in_depth.label: Signed-number addition with same signs
in_depth.description: Tests adding two numbers with the same sign by summing magnitudes and keeping the common sign.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Basic signed arithmetic is the mechanism for combining polynomial/function terms with the same sign.

in_depth.label: Signed-number addition with opposite signs
in_depth.description: Tests adding numbers with opposite signs by comparing magnitudes and retaining the sign of the larger.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Opposite-sign addition is the mechanism for like-term cancellation and subtraction in all algebraic manipulation.

in_depth.label: Signed-number subtraction as adding the opposite
in_depth.description: Tests rewriting subtraction a − b as a + (−b), which prevents sign errors when combining signed terms in expressions.
in_depth.yield_rationale: The add-the-opposite rule is the conceptual bridge to distributing negative signs in algebraic contexts; high error-prevention value.

in_depth.label: Negative signs and grouping
in_depth.description: Tests applying a negative sign outside grouping symbols by negating every term inside — numeric grouping only, not algebraic distribution.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Negating a grouped quantity is the direct predecessor to distributing a negative factor in algebraic expressions; frequent source of sign errors.

umbrellas.label: Fraction Arithmetic
umbrellas.description: Adding and subtracting fractions with common and unlike denominators, finding the LCD, and multiplying fractions — the core operations with numeric fractions.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Fraction addition and multiplication with unlike denominators are the direct numeric analogs of rational expression operations in P1; high prerequisite load.

in_depth.label: Fraction addition with common denominators
in_depth.description: Tests adding fractions with the same denominator by combining numerators while keeping the denominator unchanged.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Base case for fraction addition; prerequisite for unlike-denominator work.

in_depth.label: Fraction subtraction with common denominators
in_depth.description: Tests subtracting fractions with the same denominator by subtracting numerators; separated from addition to target sign-error patterns.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Numerator subtraction with signed fractions is a frequent error source; moderate downstream load.

in_depth.label: Least common denominator
in_depth.description: Tests finding the LCD as the LCM of fraction denominators, which is the denominator-selection prerequisite for unlike-denominator fraction arithmetic.
in_depth.yield_rationale: LCD-finding underlies adding rational expressions and clearing denominators in equation solving (F3, P2).

in_depth.label: Fraction addition with unlike denominators
in_depth.description: Tests rewriting fractions with the LCD and then adding numerators — the full unlike-denominator addition algorithm.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Unlike-denominator addition is the direct numerical analog of adding rational expressions in P1.

in_depth.label: Fraction subtraction with unlike denominators
in_depth.description: Tests subtracting fractions with different denominators after rewriting with the LCD; separated from addition because sign and order errors are distinct.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Unlike-denominator subtraction is numerically the most error-prone fraction operation; high prerequisite value.

in_depth.label: Fraction multiplication
in_depth.description: Tests multiplying numeric fractions by multiplying numerators and denominators, including signed fractions and improper fractions.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Fraction multiplication is the operation behind percent multipliers, unit-rate scaling, and coefficient manipulation.

umbrellas.label: Fraction Division and Decimal Arithmetic
umbrellas.description: Dividing fractions by reciprocal, simplifying complex fractions, and decimal addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division by place-value alignment.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Fraction division and complex fractions recur in rational expression and derivative quotient contexts; decimal arithmetic is background fluency with lower direct AP weight.

in_depth.label: Fraction division by reciprocals
in_depth.description: Tests dividing by a nonzero fraction by multiplying by its reciprocal (invert-and-multiply); numeric fractions only.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Reciprocal division is the basis for dividing rational expressions and solving equations with fractional coefficients.

in_depth.label: Numeric complex fractions
in_depth.description: Tests simplifying a fraction whose numerator, denominator, or both contain fractions, using the invert-and-multiply rule or LCD multiplication; numeric values only.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Complex-fraction structure recurs in rational function simplification and derivative quotient work.

in_depth.label: Decimal addition and subtraction alignment
in_depth.description: Tests adding and subtracting decimals by correctly aligning place values at the decimal point.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Decimal arithmetic is background fluency; less prominent than fraction arithmetic in AP contexts.

in_depth.label: Decimal multiplication place value
in_depth.description: Tests multiplying decimals by performing whole-number multiplication and then counting decimal places to position the decimal point in the product.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Decimal multiplication is a computational fluency skill; moderate background load.

in_depth.label: Decimal division place value
in_depth.description: Tests dividing decimals by adjusting the divisor to a whole number via place-value shifts, then performing division.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Decimal division is less prominent than fraction division in precalculus; lower foundation yield.

umbrellas.label: Percent Multipliers and Change
umbrellas.description: Percent as a rate per hundred, percent of a number, growth multipliers (1+r), decay multipliers (1−r), percent change, successive percent changes, and the percentage-points distinction.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Growth/decay multiplier structure is the direct numeric prototype for exponential function base b in AP Precalc 2.3; percent change and compounding feed 2.5.

in_depth.label: Percent as a rate per hundred
in_depth.description: Tests converting between percent notation and its fraction-out-of-100 or decimal form; representation only, not applied percent equations.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Percent-to-decimal conversion is the first step in computing exponential growth/decay multipliers.

in_depth.label: Percent of a number
in_depth.description: Tests finding a percent of a number by treating the percent as a decimal multiplier; numeric only, no unknown variables.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Percent-of-a-number is the arithmetic form of the exponential initial-application step.

in_depth.label: Percent increase factors
in_depth.description: Tests identifying the growth multiplier (1 + r) for a percent increase and applying it to compute the increased value.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Growth factor structure is the numeric prototype of the base b > 1 in f(x) = ab^x; directly prerequisite for AP Precalc 2.3.

in_depth.label: Percent decrease factors
in_depth.description: Tests identifying the decay multiplier (1 − r) for a percent decrease and applying it to compute the decreased value.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Decay factor structure is the numeric prototype of 0 < b < 1 in exponential decay models; directly feeds AP Precalc 2.3.

in_depth.label: Percent change
in_depth.description: Tests computing percent change as (change ÷ original) × 100; numeric change structure, not exponential modeling.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Percent change is the discrete analog of continuous growth rate; used in model-construction context items.

in_depth.label: Successive percent changes
in_depth.description: Tests that multiple percent changes compound multiplicatively, so two successive 10% increases give a factor of 1.1² not 1.2.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Multiplicative compounding is the numeric foundation of compound-growth problems in AP Precalc 2.5.

in_depth.label: Percentage points versus percent change
in_depth.description: Tests distinguishing between the arithmetic difference of two percents (percentage points) and the relative change from one percent to another (percent change).
in_depth.yield_rationale: Conceptual distinction; appears in model-interpretation items but rarely as a primary skill.

umbrellas.label: Ratios and Unit Rates
umbrellas.description: Part-to-part ratios, part-to-whole ratios, and unit rates — numeric proportional structure without algebraic equation solving.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Ratio and unit-rate structure is background for average-rate-of-change reasoning in AP Precalc 1.1–1.2; lower direct test weight than percent multipliers.

in_depth.label: Part-to-part ratios
in_depth.description: Tests writing a part-to-part ratio comparing two components of a whole; numeric ratio structure without solving proportion equations.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Ratio structure is background for rate-of-change and unit-rate reasoning.

in_depth.label: Part-to-whole ratios
in_depth.description: Tests writing a part-to-whole ratio (one component to the total), distinct from part-to-part comparisons.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Part-to-whole connects to probability-style reasoning and fraction representation.

in_depth.label: Unit rates
in_depth.description: Tests computing a unit rate by expressing a quantity per one unit of another quantity; numeric rate structure, not linear function slope.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Unit rates are the pre-algebraic form of average rate of change; feeds AP Precalc 1.1–1.2.

umbrellas.label: Real Number Ordering
umbrellas.description: Placing real numbers on the number line and ordering integers, rationals, signed numbers, mixed forms, and irrational approximations.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Number-line order supports domain/range interval notation; ordering negative numbers and mixed forms has moderate error-prevention value downstream.

in_depth.label: Number-line location
in_depth.description: Tests placing integers, fractions, decimals, and approximate irrational values at their correct positions on a number line.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Number-line fluency underpins interval and inequality notation for domains and ranges.

in_depth.label: Ordering positive rational numbers
in_depth.description: Tests comparing and ordering positive fractions, decimals, and percents by converting to a common representation.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Ordering rational numbers in different forms supports numerical reasoning in rate-of-change contexts.

in_depth.label: Ordering negative numbers
in_depth.description: Tests the counterintuitive ordering of negative numbers where a more-negative value is smaller, correcting the common misconception that larger absolute value implies larger number.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Sign-order errors in negative values persist into function evaluation and transformation-parameter interpretation.

in_depth.label: Ordering mixed real-number forms
in_depth.description: Tests ordering a list containing integers, fractions, decimals, and irrational values (e.g., √2) using decimal approximations or benchmark comparisons.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Comparing exact and approximate forms is relevant to exact-value trig contexts.

in_depth.label: Benchmark fraction comparisons
in_depth.description: Tests comparing fractions to benchmarks (0, 1/2, 1) to determine relative size without computing a common denominator.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Benchmark reasoning is an estimation heuristic; low direct AP test weight.

in_depth.label: Integer bounds for irrational numbers
in_depth.description: Tests bounding irrational square roots between consecutive integers using nearby perfect squares.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Bounding irrationals supports reasonableness checks and approximate placement of trig-related constants.

umbrellas.label: Inequality and Order Properties
umbrellas.description: Inequality symbols (<, >, ≤, ≥, =) in numeric comparisons, and the trichotomy and transitive order axioms.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Inequality symbol fluency is a notation prerequisite for interval-notation domain/range; order axioms are vocabulary-level background.

in_depth.label: Inequality symbols in numeric comparisons
in_depth.description: Tests selecting the correct symbol (<, >, ≤, ≥, =) to describe the relationship between two numeric values; comparison only, not solving inequalities.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Inequality symbol fluency is the notation prerequisite for interval-notation domain/range throughout AP Precalc.

in_depth.label: Trichotomy property of real numbers
in_depth.description: Tests that for any two real numbers, exactly one of <, =, > holds — the foundational order axiom that justifies inequality symbol usage.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Foundational axiom; rarely the direct test object above elementary level.

in_depth.label: Transitive property of order
in_depth.description: Tests that if a < b and b < c then a < c, applied to chained numeric comparisons.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Property-identification skill; minimal direct test weight in AP contexts.

umbrellas.label: Arithmetic Operation Properties
umbrellas.description: Commutativity, associativity, and distributivity for real-number arithmetic — the reordering and regrouping rules that justify term and factor rearrangement.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Distributive property is an active algebraic tool; commutativity and associativity are applied constantly when collecting terms and rearranging factors.

in_depth.label: Commutative property of addition
in_depth.description: Tests recognizing and applying a + b = b + a with numeric values; distinguishing addition commutativity from the non-commutativity of subtraction.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Property-identification vocabulary; directly applied when reordering terms in polynomial arithmetic.

in_depth.label: Commutative property of multiplication
in_depth.description: Tests recognizing and applying ab = ba with numeric values; distinguishing multiplication commutativity from the non-commutativity of division.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Used to reorder factors in polynomial and rational expression manipulation.

in_depth.label: Associative property of addition
in_depth.description: Tests recognizing that (a + b) + c = a + (b + c) with numeric values; distinguishing addition associativity from the non-associativity of subtraction.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Regrouping addends is used when collecting like terms efficiently in multi-term expressions.

in_depth.label: Associative property of multiplication
in_depth.description: Tests recognizing that (ab)c = a(bc) with numeric values; used when rearranging coefficient-and-variable factor groups.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Regrouping factors is used in monomial multiplication and exponent-law application.

in_depth.label: Distributive property with numbers
in_depth.description: Tests applying a(b + c) = ab + ac with numeric values; numeric only, not algebraic distribution with variables.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Numeric distributive property is the conceptual foundation for algebraic distribution (F2) and expanding polynomial products (F5).

in_depth.label: Noncommutativity of subtraction and division
in_depth.description: Tests that a − b ≠ b − a and a ÷ b ≠ b ÷ a in general, preventing overgeneralization of commutativity.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Error-prevention knowledge; not often tested directly at AP level but underlying many student mistakes.

in_depth.label: Nonassociativity of subtraction and division
in_depth.description: Tests that (a − b) − c ≠ a − (b − c) and (a ÷ b) ÷ c ≠ a ÷ (b ÷ c) in general.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Error-prevention for regrouping mistakes; not a direct AP exam target.

umbrellas.label: Identity, Inverse, and Zero Properties
umbrellas.description: Additive and multiplicative identity elements, additive and multiplicative inverses, zero multiplication, division by zero, and zero-as-numerator — the special-element rules for the real-number field.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Division-by-zero undefined is high-yield (domain restrictions, asymptotes); additive/multiplicative inverses are the mechanism for cancellation and equation solving.

in_depth.label: Additive identity
in_depth.description: Tests that a + 0 = a for any real number a, identifying 0 as the additive identity.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Identity property is vocabulary-level; applied implicitly in simplification but rarely the test object.

in_depth.label: Multiplicative identity
in_depth.description: Tests that a · 1 = a for any real number a, identifying 1 as the multiplicative identity.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Multiplicative identity underpins multiplying by 1 in equivalent-form rewriting, but is rarely the direct test object.

in_depth.label: Additive inverse property
in_depth.description: Tests that a + (−a) = 0 for any real number a, identifying additive inverses as opposites.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Additive inverse is the mechanism for cancellation in like-term collection and equation solving.

in_depth.label: Multiplicative inverse property
in_depth.description: Tests that a · (1/a) = 1 for any nonzero real number a, identifying the reciprocal as the multiplicative inverse.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Reciprocal/multiplicative inverse is the mechanism for division and for isolating variables in one-step equations.

in_depth.label: Zero multiplication property
in_depth.description: Tests that a · 0 = 0 for any real number a; numeric zero behavior, not the zero-product property for equations.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Zero multiplication is background; the zero-product property for equations lives in F5 (polynomials).

in_depth.label: Division by zero is undefined
in_depth.description: Tests recognizing that a ÷ 0 is undefined for any real a, because no real number times 0 can produce a nonzero result.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Division-by-zero is the foundation for vertical asymptotes in rational functions and domain restrictions throughout AP Precalc.

in_depth.label: Zero divided by a nonzero number
in_depth.description: Tests that 0 ÷ a = 0 for any nonzero a, distinguishing this from the undefined case of dividing by zero.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Zero-numerator reasoning supports rational function zero identification (P1 topic 1.8).

umbrellas.label: Closure Properties of Number Sets
umbrellas.description: Closure of ℝ, ℤ, and ℚ under the four basic operations; nonclosure patterns of irrational numbers.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Closure is structural vocabulary at AP tier; irrational nonclosure supports exact-form reasoning. Low direct exam weight.

in_depth.label: Closure properties of real numbers
in_depth.description: Tests that ℝ is closed under addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division by nonzero reals, meaning results stay within ℝ.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Closure is a structural property; vocabulary-level at AP tier.

in_depth.label: Closure properties of integers
in_depth.description: Tests that ℤ is closed under addition, subtraction, and multiplication but not division (3 ÷ 2 ∉ ℤ).
in_depth.yield_rationale: Integer closure contrasts with rational closure; background knowledge.

in_depth.label: Closure properties of rational numbers
in_depth.description: Tests that ℚ is closed under the four basic operations (excluding division by zero), so any arithmetic result of rationals is rational.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Rational closure underpins the fact that polynomial and rational function outputs at rational inputs are rational.

in_depth.label: Nonclosure patterns of irrational numbers
in_depth.description: Tests that irrational numbers are not closed under any basic operation (e.g., √2 · √2 = 2 is rational), requiring case-by-case reasoning.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Irrational nonclosure supports reasoning about exact-form simplification in radical and trig contexts.

umbrellas.label: Numeric Expression Structure
umbrellas.description: Order of operations, grouping symbols (parentheses, brackets, fraction bars, absolute value bars), exponent precedence, and negative-base parentheses in purely numeric expressions.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Order-of-operations and negative-base precedence are active error sources in every category; fraction-bar and absolute-value grouping recur in rational function and piecewise function evaluation.

in_depth.label: Precedence of multiplication and division
in_depth.description: Tests that multiplication and division are performed before addition and subtraction in numeric expressions without grouping symbols.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Multiplication-before-addition precedence is the most commonly violated order-of-operations rule in student work.

in_depth.label: Left-to-right order for same-precedence operations
in_depth.description: Tests that operations at the same precedence level (e.g., multiplication and division) are evaluated left to right.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Left-to-right rule prevents errors in multi-operation simplification; frequently tested in expression evaluation.

in_depth.label: Parentheses and grouping symbols
in_depth.description: Tests treating grouped quantities as a single unit — evaluating the expression inside parentheses, brackets, or braces before combining with the rest.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Grouping-symbol fluency is directly required for evaluating functions at specific inputs and for fraction-bar interpretation.

in_depth.label: Nested grouping symbols
in_depth.description: Tests working from the innermost group outward when multiple levels of grouping symbols are present in a numeric expression.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Nested-group evaluation recurs in composed functions and in rational expressions with grouped numerators/denominators.

in_depth.label: Fraction bar as grouping
in_depth.description: Tests interpreting the fraction bar as grouping the entire numerator and the entire denominator separately before dividing.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Fraction-bar grouping is a prerequisite for reading and evaluating rational expressions and average-rate-of-change formulas.

in_depth.label: Absolute value bars as grouping
in_depth.description: Tests evaluating the expression inside absolute value bars before applying the absolute value; numeric grouping only, not absolute value equations.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Absolute value bar grouping is prerequisite for piecewise function evaluation and distance computations.

in_depth.label: Numeric exponent precedence
in_depth.description: Tests that exponentiation is performed before multiplication, division, addition, and subtraction unless overridden by grouping; covers order of operations only, not exponent laws.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Exponent-before-multiply precedence is the second most common order-of-operations error and recurs in polynomial evaluation.

in_depth.label: Negative bases and parentheses
in_depth.description: Tests distinguishing −3² = −9 (negation applied after squaring) from (−3)² = 9 (negative included in the base), based on parenthesis scope.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Negative-base ambiguity is one of the most frequent arithmetic errors in polynomial evaluation, function substitution, and transformation work.

umbrellas.label: Approximation, Estimation, and Bounds
umbrellas.description: Rounding to place value, truncation, compatible-number estimation, bounds from rounded values, order-of-magnitude reasoning, and reasonableness checks for numeric results.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Reasonableness checking and order-of-magnitude reasoning support model-validation tasks in AP Precalc 2.6; not a focal tested skill at AP level.

in_depth.label: Rounding to place value
in_depth.description: Tests rounding a decimal or whole number to a specified place value using the standard look-at-the-next-digit rule.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Rounding is used when reporting decimal approximations of irrational values and regression outputs.

in_depth.label: Truncation versus rounding
in_depth.description: Tests distinguishing truncation (cutting off digits) from rounding (which may increase the last retained digit), showing that results can differ.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Truncation vs. rounding is a precision concept; low direct AP exam weight.

in_depth.label: Compatible-number estimation
in_depth.description: Tests replacing inconvenient numbers with nearby compatible values to facilitate quick mental estimation of sums, products, or quotients.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Estimation strategy; supports reasonableness checks but not a direct AP exam skill.

in_depth.label: Bounds from rounded numbers
in_depth.description: Tests identifying the interval [lower, upper) of possible original values that would round to a given approximation.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Bounds reasoning is used in model-validation and error-analysis contexts (AP Precalc 2.6); moderate but targeted.

in_depth.label: Order-of-magnitude estimation
in_depth.description: Tests describing the approximate size of a number by identifying the nearest power of 10; estimation-scale reasoning, not exponent-law computation.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Order-of-magnitude thinking supports semi-log plot interpretation in AP Precalc 2.15.

in_depth.label: Reasonableness checks for numeric results
in_depth.description: Tests verifying a computed answer against estimates of its expected size, sign, and magnitude to detect arithmetic errors.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Reasonableness checking is a mathematical practice skill assessed in FRQ justification parts; moderate foundation load.
